message:
feat(core,abi): port parse_addr_binary IP text→bytes to core::resolv (bd-pab-epic)

Lifts ~14 lines of inline IP text→binary parsing out of unistd_abi.rs
into core::resolv. Completes the inet text↔binary conversion family;
sits alongside the existing inet_pton / inet_ntop / inet_aton /
inet_addr in core::inet.

New core surface:
  pub enum AddrFamily { Inet4, Inet6 }
  impl AddrFamily { pub fn addr_len(self) -> usize }
  pub fn parse_addr_binary(text: &str) -> Option<([u8; 16], AddrFamily, usize)>

The libc::AF_INET / AF_INET6 dependency is replaced by a typed enum
that the abi shim maps at the boundary.

abi changes:
  - parse_addr_binary becomes a 9-line shim that delegates to core
    and maps AddrFamily::Inet4/Inet6 to libc::AF_INET/AF_INET6.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-pab-2):
  - 10 new core::resolv unit tests pass (IPv4 loopback / arbitrary /
    zero / max-octets, IPv6 abbreviated / full / v4-mapped, garbage
    rejection, trailing-text rejection)
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-pab-epic.
